vulnerability.reason: Reference describes over-reliance due to automation bias, generated describes lack of means to detect unreliable estimates without baselines – related but different sensitivity condition.
vulnerability.reference: Clinicians over-rely on AI recommendations due to automation bias in high-stakes, time-pressured ICU settings.
vulnerability.generated: Clinicians lack means to detect unreliable RL value estimates, trusting recommendations without validated baselines or override protocols.

impact.reason: Both affected party (sepsis patients) and damage type (mortality/morbidity, equivalent to deterioration and preventable mortality) are present.
impact.reference: Increased mortality and morbidity from inappropriate sepsis interventions.
impact.generated: Sepsis patients receive harmful treatment choices, causing clinical deterioration and preventable mortality.

dose_response.reason: The generated arc omits the step where biased estimates enable unreliable policy recommendations, instead directly linking estimates to exposure triggering inability to detect – the causal chain is partially preserved but misses a key link.
dose_response.reference: Biased or high-variance off-policy evaluation estimates enable unreliable policy recommendations, which in ICU sepsis treatment optimization leads to harmful treatment choices when clinicians over-rely on AI guidance.
dose_response.generated: Biased and high-variance off-policy evaluation estimates, through exposure of clinicians and patients to AI-guided ICU treatment plans, trigger clinicians' inability to detect unreliable value estimates without validated baselines, leading to harmful sepsis treatment choices causing deterioration and preventable mortality.
